---
title: "Artifact Graphs, Audit Logs, and Replay"
chapter_id: "artifact-graphs-audit-logs-and-replay"
part_id: "planning-memory-reasoning-execution"
status: "conceptual"
draft_maturity: "v0.2 manuscript draft"
last_updated: "2026-06-25"
primary_sources:
  - "talos"
  - "viea"
  - "cognitive_compilation"
  - "spinoza_composer"
  - "genesiscode"
  - "cognitive_loop_closure"
  - "moecot"
evidence_level: "argument"
claim_label: "Design rationale"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Appendix C now records exact source-note mappings for the core claim; support remains at argument until artifact replay, audit reconstruction, or completeness harnesses exist."
  - "Protocol schema/fixture validation and narrow Lean provenance predicates are implemented; no artifact-graph replay, audit reconstruction, or produced-artifact completeness harness has been run for this chapter."
  - "No support-state promotion is implied by this drafting pass."
---

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `artifact-graphs-audit-logs-and-replay` |
| Part | Part II - Planning, Memory, Reasoning, and Execution |
| Status | conceptual |
| Manuscript maturity | v0.2 manuscript draft |
| Last updated | 2026-06-25 |
| Primary source records | `talos`, `viea`, `cognitive_compilation`, `spinoza_composer`, `genesiscode`, `cognitive_loop_closure`, `moecot` |
| Claim label | Design rationale |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source queue | primary: `talos`, `viea`; supporting: `cognitive_compilation`, `spinoza_composer`, `genesiscode`, `cognitive_loop_closure`; connector/recovery: `moecot` |
| Source loading state | source notes: `talos`, `viea`, `cognitive_compilation`, `spinoza_composer`, `genesiscode`, `cognitive_loop_closure`, `moecot`; raw cache: `talos`, `viea`, `cognitive_compilation`, `spinoza_composer`, `genesiscode`, `cognitive_loop_closure`; connector/recovery: `moecot` |
| Test state | Artifact graph fixture validation implemented; finite provenance predicates implemented in `AsiStackProofs.ArtifactGraph`; replay, audit-reconstruction, and produced-artifact tests remain planned. |

## Drafting guardrail

This chapter treats artifacts as provenance-bearing work products, not as automatic evidence. The repo validates an artifact-graph fixture and now proves two finite Lean predicates over provenance records, but it has not run a replay engine, audit reconstruction harness, produced-artifact checker, or provenance completeness service for this chapter.

## Problem

The stack needs durable artifacts and replayable traces so work can be inspected, reused, tested, and improved.

Typed jobs produce outputs. The stack needs those outputs to become artifacts with stable identities, provenance, audit events, replay metadata, claim/test links, and known replay limits. Otherwise every result is an isolated file or chat answer with no reliable path back to its causes.

Talos treats evidence, logs, and replay as execution primitives. GenesisCode adds effect logs, semantic patches, provenance hashes, and obligation artifacts. Cognitive Loop Closure uses repeated artifact traces as the substrate for future tools.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

If outputs are not tied to inputs, tools, context, claims, and logs, later verification and improvement cannot know what happened.

Untraceable output cannot support promoted claims. Non-replayable workflows cannot be debugged or improved. Provenance gaps make it impossible to tell whether an artifact came from a source, a tool, a model guess, a human edit, or a stale context packet.

The artifact graph is the system's memory of work. It does not guarantee truth, but it makes verification possible by preserving what happened.

## Core Claim

[artifact-graphs-audit-logs-and-replay.core, label: Design rationale, support: argument] Execution should produce an artifact graph with audit logs, provenance, replay metadata, and links to claims and tests.

The claim remains at `argument` support. Talos supplies execution evidence and replay pressure; VIEA supplies artifact and feedback ledgers; Cognitive Compilation supplies build-graph and repair-locality pressure; Spinoza Composer supplies state/history preservation patterns; GenesisCode supplies effect logs, provenance hashes, and obligations; Cognitive Loop Closure explains why traces become procedural memory.

### Claim-source mapping status

Appendix C now maps this artifact-graph claim to all assigned source notes. The mappings support provenance-bearing artifacts, audit logs, replay metadata, trace preservation, and improvement substrate discipline, not a completed replay engine or artifact graph service.

| Source | What it supports | Limit |
|---|---|---|
| `talos` | Typed jobs, delivery, evidence records, audit logs, replay, source allow-listing, blind handles, tests, residuals, regression coverage, and forgotten-failure prevention. | No Talos artifact graph, audit reconstruction, replay run, benchmark, or security result has been reproduced here. |
| `viea` | Artifact graphs for claims, requirements, critiques, releases, feedback, tools, benchmarks, residuals, verification ledgers, runtime execution, and feedback loops. | No completed VIEA deployment, runtime trace, artifact graph service, or benchmark result exists here. |
| `cognitive_compilation` | Build-graph traceability through source plans, typed semantic IR, dependencies, validation requirements, scheduling, target lowering, localized repair, and compiler-style metrics. | No working compiler, trace suite, repair-locality benchmark, or empirical ablation exists here. |
| `spinoza_composer` | World graphs, belief states, causal history, scope tags, narrative trees, verified logical state, disclosures, exports, baselines, and evaluation plans. | No composer implementation, continuity benchmark, extraction validation, or render-from-verified-state test has been run. |
| `genesiscode` | Canonical IR, semantic patches, provenance hashes, deterministic effect logs, replay/audit, obligations, and privileged protocol seals. | No GenesisCode kernel, semantic patch checker, deterministic replay system, or proof artifact exists here. |
| `cognitive_loop_closure` | Execution traces as procedural-memory substrate through logging, loop detection, tool cards, verification, registries, monitoring, lifecycle states, and retirement discipline. | No local loop detector, trace miner, synthesized tool, or verification harness has been executed. |
| `moecot` | Runtime-reference ledgers, replay, handoff, readiness gates, benchmark artifacts, promotion blockers, and residual tracking. | Runtime artifacts, ledgers, logs, and benchmark records have not been imported, inspected, or reproduced here. |

## Mechanism

The artifact graph is execution memory. A typed job may finish, fail, or be retired, but the stack still needs to know what was produced, which source/context/tool path produced it, which claims and tests it touches, and whether any part of the path can be replayed. Talos gives the audit/replay pressure, VIEA gives the artifact-and-feedback ledger, GenesisCode gives provenance hashes and effect logs, Cognitive Compilation gives build-graph traceability, and Cognitive Loop Closure explains why repeated traces become future tools.

An Artifact Graph Record is the durable node at that boundary. It is not a claim that the artifact is true. It is the minimum evidence surface that lets verification, memory, replay, and procedural learning reason about the artifact without relying on chat history.

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  A["Typed job"] --> B["Artifact node"]
  B --> C["Source refs"]
  B --> D["Context refs"]
  B --> E["Tool / adapter refs"]
  B --> F["Audit events"]
  B --> G["Replay metadata"]
  B --> H["Claim + test links"]
  F --> I["Evidence ledger"]
  G --> I
  H --> I
```

- Record artifacts as nodes with source, job, context, tool, claim, test, and evidence links.
- Capture audit events, replay metadata, environment assumptions, provenance status, and replay limits.
- Treat incomplete provenance as a residual rather than quietly using the artifact as evidence.
- Use verified and failed traces as memory for later regression suites, procedural tools, and repair work.

Artifacts should have stable IDs and versioned revisions. Audit logs should be append-only or versioned. Replay metadata should declare what can be replayed, what cannot, and which environment assumptions matter.

The graph is also an improvement substrate. Repeated traces can become procedural memory. Failed traces can become residuals. Verified artifacts can become regression coverage.

## Interfaces

The interface is the Artifact Graph Record.

- VCM references artifacts.
- Evidence consumes logs.
- Procedural memory mines repeated traces.

Minimum fields:

- `artifact_id`
- `artifact_type`
- `parent_job`
- `source_refs`
- `context_refs`
- `tool_refs`
- `claim_refs`
- `test_refs`
- `audit_events`
- `replay_metadata`
- `environment_assumptions`
- `provenance_status`
- `replay_limits`

VCM references artifacts as context cells. Evidence consumes logs and test links. Procedural memory mines repeated traces. Claim ledgers decide whether an artifact can support a claim.

## Invariants

- Artifacts have stable identities.
- Audit logs are append-only or versioned.
- Replay limits are declared.

An artifact with missing required provenance cannot support a promoted claim. It can still exist, but it should be labeled as incomplete, not evidence-ready.

## Failure modes

- Untraceable output.
- Non-replayable workflow.
- Provenance gaps.

Untraceable output becomes a residual. Non-replayable workflows must declare replay limits. Provenance gaps block evidence promotion until repaired or explicitly bounded.

## Minimal implementation

An artifact graph schema with job, source, context, and evidence edges.

The smallest useful implementation is an `artifact_graph_record` schema and fixture. It validates parent job, source/context/tool/claim/test refs, audit events, replay metadata, environment assumptions, provenance status, and replay limits.

That fixture validates the shape of an artifact node. `AsiStackProofs.ArtifactGraph` adds a bounded predicate requiring produced artifacts to carry parent-job and source/context references, plus a bounded promotion-blocking predicate for missing required provenance. This does not prove the referenced render, replay, audit path, or provenance completeness of any produced artifact.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Artifact graph fixture validation | Check that the artifact fixture matches the public schema and declares parent job, source/context/tool/claim/test refs, audit events, replay metadata, environment assumptions, provenance status, and replay limits. | implemented by protocol validation; run in this pass |
| Artifact traceability predicate | Check that produced artifact records expose parent job and source/context references. | implemented in `AsiStackProofs.ArtifactGraph`; checked by Lean build |
| Missing provenance promotion predicate | Check that missing required provenance blocks promoted-claim support. | implemented in `AsiStackProofs.ArtifactGraph`; checked by Lean build |
| Audit reconstruction test | Check that audit events are sufficient to reconstruct the execution path. | planned; not run |
| Replay metadata completeness test | Check that replay limits and environment assumptions are declared. | planned; not run |

Fixture-shape validation and two bounded Lean predicates are implemented. The remaining tests require produced artifacts, audit logs, and replay attempts that can be inspected.

### Formalization hooks

| Tag | Module | Target | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `lean:artifacts.graph.operational_invariant` | `AsiStackProofs.ArtifactGraph` | Every produced artifact records its parent job and source/context references. | implemented |
| `lean:artifacts.graph.failure_blocks_promotion` | `AsiStackProofs.ArtifactGraph` | An artifact with missing required provenance cannot support a promoted claim. | implemented |

These proof hooks are implemented as finite-record predicates. They do not prove replayability, audit reconstruction, or artifact-type-specific completeness.

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Layer | Planned use | Readiness |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `talos` | Talos Protocol | labor_execution_os | AI labor OS. Deterministic cognitive manufacturing, typed jobs, control planes, auditability, tool isolation. | source note available; local raw cache available |
| `viea` | Verified Intent-to-Execution Architecture | whole_stack_execution_spine | Keystone source. Human intent -> command contracts -> artifacts -> routing -> runtime targets -> verification -> deployment -> feedback. | source note available; local raw cache available |
| `cognitive_compilation` | Cognitive Compilation | planning_semantic_ir | Compiler framing for LLM-centered planning, semantic IR, target compilation, incremental repair. | source note available; local raw cache available |
| `spinoza_composer` | Spinoza Composer / Spinoza Trinity | reasoning_media_compliance | Narrative/compliance operating system variant. Use as applied Spinoza. | source note available; local raw cache available |
| `genesiscode` | GenesisCode | executable_specification | Tiny pure calculus + obligations + provenance for auditable AI-symbiotic programming. | source note available; local raw cache available |
| `cognitive_loop_closure` | Cognitive Loop Closure | procedural_memory | Repeated cognition should become procedural memory / verified tools. | source note available; local raw cache available |
| `moecot` | MoECOT-Agent Architecture Whitepaper | implementation_reference | Concrete implementation evidence: governed low-parameter multi-core runtime, readiness gates, ledgers, replay. | source note available; connector or recovery required |

The crosswalk identifies sources for artifact provenance, audit, replay, and loop-closure discipline. It does not claim local replayability until a replay record exists.

## Summary

Artifact Graphs, Audit Logs, and Replay turns outputs into durable work products. Each artifact should carry identity, parent job, source/context/tool links, claim/test links, audit events, replay metadata, environment assumptions, provenance status, and replay limits.

This is how execution becomes memory without becoming myth: the artifact graph records what happened, what can be replayed, and what remains uncertain. The next chapter moves to the most dangerous boundary in the execution layer: the adapter that turns a job into an external effect.
